profile.name: Jennifer
profile.role_in_household: Primary client; tends to coordinate family logistics and is the more detail-oriented planner in financial conversations.
profile.financial_literacy_level: Upper-intermediate: comfortable with retirement accounts and basic investing concepts, asks clarifying questions on taxes/fees, and likes to reconcile numbers to statements.
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Moderate but structured: willing to take market risk for long-term growth, yet prefers guardrails (diversification, clear downside framing) and gets uneasy when allocations feel 'too concentrated' or unclear.
profile.communication_style: Direct and organized; will reference specific figures from memory or notes, corrects small inaccuracies politely, and uses occasional hedges like 'I think' or 'if I’m remembering right' when unsure. Prefers agendas and summaries.
emotional_tendencies: mild anxiety when plans feel vague
relief when action items are clearly assigned
profile.decision_making_style: Analytical and checklist-driven; wants to understand tradeoffs, then decides once she sees a coherent plan and timeline.
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Cautiously open: willing to engage and share details, but trust increases when the advisor demonstrates accuracy, transparency on fees, and proactive tax awareness.
internal_biases: loss aversion (prefers avoiding big drawdowns over chasing maximum returns)
anchoring to account balances and recent market performance
hidden_concerns: whether their current savings/investing pace truly supports retirement timing given two children
whether insurance costs are 'worth it' versus investing more, even if she feels safer having coverage

profile.name: William
profile.role_in_household: Spouse/partner; brings most questions about the taxable brokerage and cash, but often defers to Jennifer on final decisions unless he feels strongly.
profile.financial_literacy_level: Intermediate: understands brokerage basics and cash management, but mixes up terminology (gross vs net, premium vs benefit, IRA vs 401(k)) and prefers simplified explanations tied to outcomes.
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Moderate with a tilt toward opportunistic: comfortable holding a sizable taxable account and may be tempted by 'what’s working now,' but pulls back quickly if headlines feel scary; prefers keeping a noticeable cash buffer.
profile.communication_style: Conversational and informal; speaks in round numbers ('about four hundred-ish'), uses fillers ('uh', 'you know'), and occasionally interrupts with a practical question. Admits when he doesn’t remember exact details and asks for the 'bottom line.'
emotional_tendencies: frustration when discussions get too technical
profile.decision_making_style: Heuristic and outcome-focused; prefers 2–3 clear options with pros/cons, then aligns with Jennifer after hearing how it affects cash flow and flexibility.
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Neutral-to-positive: trusts advisors who are straightforward and responsive; skepticism rises if recommendations feel salesy or overly complex.
internal_biases: mental accounting (separates 'cash' as untouchable even when it may be investable)
hidden_concerns: fear of making a mistake in the taxable account that creates an unexpected tax bill
health-related worry he doesn’t lead with (especially around habits), which can make him more sensitive to protection and retirement timing discussions
